title: English Language Unity Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill makes English the official language of the United States. It says that all government work and business must be done in English. People who want to become US citizens will need to meet English language requirements. The bill also creates rules for how to understand laws that are written in English but might be confusing.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law may be called the English Language Unity Act of 2025.

sections.title: Section 2  Findings
sections.text: Congress finds and declares the following facts. The United States is made up of people from many different ethnic, cultural, and language backgrounds. Throughout American history, the English language has been a common bond that connects citizens who come from different backgrounds. States have the power to make English their official language and to promote English within their state, as long as they follow the rules in the Constitution and their own state laws.

sections.title: Section 3  English as Official Language of the United States
sections.text: This section adds a new chapter to Title 4 of the United States Code called Official Language. The official language of the United States is English. Federal government representatives must work to preserve and enhance the role of English as the official language. This includes encouraging more opportunities for people to learn English. All official functions of the United States government must be conducted in English. Official functions are those that bind the government, are required by law, or can be reviewed by the press or public. This rule applies to all laws, public meetings, regulations, publications, orders, actions, programs, and policies. However, this rule does not apply to teaching languages, requirements under the law for people with disabilities, actions needed for national security or international relations, actions that protect public health and safety, census activities, actions that protect crime victims or defendants, or using foreign terms that have special meanings. All people who become United States citizens should be able to read and understand the Declaration of Independence, the Constitution, and United States laws written in English. All citizenship ceremonies must be conducted in English. Nothing in this chapter stops members of Congress or government workers from talking informally in other languages as long as their official duties are done in English. This chapter does not limit the use of Native Alaskan or Native American languages or discourage anyone from learning other languages. People who are harmed by violations of this chapter can file a lawsuit to get proper relief.

sections.title: Section 4  General Rules of Construction for English Language Texts of the Laws of the United States
sections.text: This section adds a new rule to Title 1 of the United States Code about how to understand laws written in English. English language requirements and workplace policies in both government and private jobs should be assumed to follow United States laws. When the English text of United States laws is unclear or could mean different things, courts should interpret the law in a way that does not take away rights that people keep for themselves and that leaves powers with the states or the people, following the last two amendments in the Bill of Rights.

sections.title: Section 5  Implementing Regulations
sections.text: Within one hundred eighty days after this law is passed, the Secretary of Homeland Security must create and publish a proposed rule for testing English language skills of people who want to become citizens. The rule must be based on two principles. First, all United States citizens should be able to read and generally understand the Declaration of Independence, the Constitution, and United States laws written in English. Second, any exceptions to this standard should only be allowed in extraordinary circumstances.

sections.title: Section 6  Effective Date
sections.text: The changes made by sections three and four will begin one hundred eighty days after this law is passed.
